sources.name: IFDB game page — 9:05 by Adam Cadre

questions.q: The phone won't stop ringing and a voice is yelling at me to get to work. What now?
hints: The voice on the phone wants you moving fast — but fast isn't the same as thorough.
Everything in this house will let you hurry past it. That doesn't mean you should.
Before you do anything else, get up and take a good look around.
ANSWER PHONE, then STAND. Then look around the room instead of rushing out.

questions.q: Is there anywhere in this room I haven't really looked?
hints: A bedroom has more to it than the parts you can see standing up.
Think about what's usually out of sight, low down, easy to ignore on a rushed morning.
Try looking underneath something large and low to the floor.
LOOK UNDER BED.

questions.q: Something about last night feels off. What do I notice if I check myself?
hints: Whatever happened last night, your own body might remember more than you do.
X ME.

questions.q: I need to get cleaned up and dressed before I can leave. What's the routine?
hints: You'll need to be undressed before the shower will do you any good.
The shower won't let you carry anything in with you, either — set everything down first.
REMOVE WATCH, REMOVE CLOTHES, DROP ALL, then ENTER SHOWER. Afterward TAKE WATCH, WEAR WATCH, head back to the Bedroom (N), GET ALL FROM TABLE, OPEN DRESSER, GET CLOTHES, WEAR CLOTHES.

questions.q: I'm dressed. How do I get out to the car?
hints: This house has more than one door — be specific about which one you mean.
From the Living Room, OPEN FRONT DOOR, go S to the Driveway, then UNLOCK CAR and ENTER CAR — the keys you're already holding do it.

questions.q: The car asks me questions as I drive. How should I answer them?
hints: One of these questions offers you a quick, quiet way out of town. Taking it ends things early — and thinly.
The phone call at the start already told you where you work. Has the drive actually offered you a way to go there?
Skip the freeway on-ramp, and say yes the next time somewhere familiar comes up.
Answer NO to the freeway question, then YES when Loungent Technologies is mentioned.

questions.q: There's a locked employee entrance with a card slot by the door. How do I get in?
hints: You're carrying more than one piece of identification. Only one of them means anything to this door.
Open the wallet and see what's actually in it.
OPEN WALLET, TAKE ID, then INSERT ID IN SLOT — not the driver's license.

questions.q: There's a cubicle here marked with a name. What's expected of me at my desk?
hints: Someone left something for you here, and it sounds urgent. Read it before you do anything else.
The note wants a form signed and returned immediately — take it seriously.
ENTER CUBICLE, READ NOTE, then TAKE FORM, TAKE PEN, SIGN FORM.

questions.q: I've signed the form. What do I do with it?
hints: The note is signed with initials. Someone in this building matches them.
There's an office door with a fuller name on it, just west of the cubicle.
Fair warning: delivering it ends the story — there's no undoing what happens in that office.
OUT, then W into the office marked "MATTHEW BOWMAN."

questions.q: This all feels very ordinary — showering, dressing, driving to work. Am I missing something?
hints: The game keeps insisting you're late and should hurry. Insisting that hard is itself worth noticing.
Try not rushing. Look under things, read what's handed to you, and take the option that actually leads somewhere rather than the one that gets you out of town fastest.
The 'quick escape' answers exist, and they end the game quickly too — with almost nothing in them. The version of this story worth seeing is the slower one.
